title: AI Proposal & Pitch Deck Tools for Freelancers in 2026: Closing Clients Faster with Better.Proposals vs PandaDoc vs Qwilr
description: Better Proposals starts at $19/mo, PandaDoc at $19/mo, Qwilr at $35/user. We compare pricing, automation, and who wins for solopreneurs closing deals in 2026.
tldr: Better Proposals ($19/mo, unlimited seats, 110 docs/year) wins for budget-conscious solopreneurs. PandaDoc ($19/seat/mo, 110 docs/year) matches the price but charges per seat. Qwilr ($35/user/mo) costs more but offers interactive quotes and stronger HubSpot automation. Proposify ($19/user/mo annual, 10 sends/month) is cheapest but severely limits sends. Bonsai pricing unavailable; contact vendor.

body.text: Freelancers lose deals in email threads—version chaos, missed follow-ups, unsigned PDFs. Proposal software fixes that: web-based delivery, real-time tracking, embedded e-signature, and payment collection in one flow. Better Proposals, PandaDoc, Qwilr, Proposify, and Bonsai all claim to close deals faster. Pricing spans $0–$59/user/mo. The right pick depends on send volume, CRM stack, and whether you need interactive quotes or just clean templates with signatures.

We reviewed vendor pricing pages, feature lists, and integration documentation published as of May 2026. Pricing is quoted exactly as listed; features not documented on public pages are omitted. No fabricated testing—conclusions are drawn from published specs, not personal case counts or conversion metrics.

Better Proposals positions itself as a Word/PDF replacement: drag-and-drop editor, real-time collaboration, web-link delivery, tracking, e-signature, and post-signature client onboarding. Per the vendor's pricing page, the Starter plan is $19/mo billed annually (110 docs/year, unlimited seats). Key features include audit trail, bulk import, in-line comments, and 24/7 email/chat support. Custom branding, CRM integrations (unspecified which CRMs), deal rooms, and approval workflows arrive in the Business plan at $49/seat/mo billed annually (unlimited documents). The vendor claims 10,000+ users and emphasizes 'no design skill needed' templating.

body.name: Better Proposals
body.price: $19/mo Starter (110 docs/year, unlimited seats, billed annually); $49/seat/mo Business (unlimited docs, billed annually)
pros: Unlimited seats on Starter—no per-user charges for small teams
110 docs/year sufficient for most solopreneurs at $19/mo entry price
Real-time collaboration and tracking included at Starter tier
Post-signature onboarding workflow (form collection, payment, calendar booking)
cons: Business plan required for CRM integrations—$49/seat/mo jump
CRM list not detailed on pricing page; unclear which platforms supported
110-doc annual cap may hit limit for high-volume freelancers
No pricing transparency for Enterprise or custom needs

body.text: PandaDoc offers document generation, CPQ (configure-price-quote), deal rooms, and e-signature. Vendor pricing page lists Free (60 docs/year, unlimited seats), Starter ($19/seat/mo billed annually, 110 docs/year, unlimited seats), Business ($49/seat/mo billed annually, unlimited docs), and Enterprise (custom pricing, per-seat or per-document). Starter includes audit trail, bulk import, in-line comments, and 24/7 support. Business adds CRM integrations (HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive noted), custom branding, deal rooms, and approval workflows. Enterprise unlocks CPQ, workflow automation, smart content, SSO, team workspaces, and API. Overages: Starter $2/doc annually, $3.50/doc monthly; Business $2.60/doc monthly. Per-seat pricing applies from Starter upward.

body.name: PandaDoc
body.price: $19/seat/mo Starter (110 docs/year, billed annually); $49/seat/mo Business (unlimited docs, billed annually); Enterprise custom pricing
pros: Strong CRM ecosystem—HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive integrations at Business tier
CPQ and workflow automation at Enterprise level for scaling teams
Deal rooms and approval workflows included in Business
API access and SSO in Enterprise for custom integrations
cons: Per-seat pricing from Starter—$19/seat adds up for multi-person freelance collectives
CRM integrations locked behind $49/seat Business tier
Free tier's 60 docs/year insufficient for active freelancers
Overage pricing ($2–$3.50/doc) can surprise at Starter tier

body.text: Qwilr emphasizes interactive web pages over static PDFs: drag-and-drop editor, dynamic content (ROI calculators, forms, video embeds), quote tables, tiered pricing plans, QwilrPay for embedded payments, and e-signature. Per vendor pricing page, Business is $35/user/mo (monthly billing) with unlimited document creation, e-sign, QwilrPay (0.09% application fee per transaction), pricing/quote tables, page analytics, and HubSpot CRM integration. Enterprise is $59/user/mo billed annually (10-user minimum) and adds account manager, custom branding, team permissions, identity verification, domain-restricted access, Salesforce/Pipedrive/Zoho CRM integrations, and reduced QwilrPay fee (0.05%). Business plan supports HubSpot, Pipedrive, Zoho; Enterprise adds Salesforce.

body.name: Qwilr
body.price: $35/user/mo Business (monthly billing, unlimited docs); $59/user/mo Enterprise (annual billing, 10-user min)
pros: Interactive quote tables and tiered pricing plans—strong for upselling optional services
QwilrPay embedded payments with low application fees (0.09% Business, 0.05% Enterprise)
HubSpot automation at Business tier; Salesforce at Enterprise
Dynamic content: ROI calculators, forms, video embeds included
cons: $35/user/mo entry price higher than Better Proposals and PandaDoc Starter
Enterprise 10-user minimum ($590/mo annual commitment) steep for solopreneurs
No document send cap listed, but per-user pricing adds cost for small teams
Custom fonts and domain-restricted access Enterprise-only

body.text: Proposify markets to consultants, landscaping, janitorial, and construction verticals. Vendor pricing page lists Basic ($19/user/mo annual, $29/user/mo monthly, 10 sends/month, up to 3 templates), Team ($41/user/mo annual, $49/user/mo quarterly, 30 sends/month, up to 10 templates), and Business (custom pricing, starts at $3,900/year, 75 sends/month, unlimited templates). Overage rates: Basic $0.50/send, Team $0.30/send, Business $0.75/send. Basic includes e-signatures and document tracking. Team adds unlimited sending (30/month base), document analytics, integrations/automations, and custom fields/variables. Business adds unlimited templates, user roles/permissions, SSO, API, multiple workspaces, and onboarding options. Drafting and editing unlimited on all plans; sends are counted when shared with clients.

body.name: Proposify
body.price: $19/user/mo Basic (10 sends/month, annual billing); $41/user/mo Team (30 sends/month, annual billing); Business starts at $3,900/year (75 sends/month)
pros: $19/user/mo annual matches PandaDoc Starter, undercutting monthly pricing
Overage rate transparency: $0.30–$0.75/send depending on tier
Team tier includes integrations and analytics at $41/user/mo
Business tier API and SSO for scaling operations
cons: 10 sends/month cap on Basic plan restrictive—most solopreneurs send >10 proposals/month
Template limits (3 on Basic, 10 on Team) constrain service-line diversity
$3,900/year Business entry price high for solo operators
Overage fees add up fast if send volume exceeds tier cap

body.text: Bonsai is known for bundling proposals, contracts, invoicing, time tracking, and client CRM in one freelancer suite. Vendor pricing page was unreachable in the research bundle; no pricing, feature list, or integration details available. Contact vendor directly for current plans and rates.

body.title: Bonsai Pricing Unavailable
body.text: No public pricing page reachable as of May 2026. If you need an all-in-one freelancer suite (proposals + invoicing + contracts + time tracking), request a quote from Bonsai directly.

items: Budget solopreneurs sending <110 proposals/year: Better Proposals Starter ($19/mo, unlimited seats, 110 docs/year) or Proposify Basic ($19/user/mo annual, 10 sends/month + $0.50/overage)—but watch Proposify's 10-send cap.
Freelancers needing HubSpot automation and interactive quotes: Qwilr Business ($35/user/mo) for quote tables, tiered pricing, and embedded payments at 0.09% fee.
Teams scaling with Salesforce or Pipedrive: PandaDoc Business ($49/seat/mo, unlimited docs) or Qwilr Enterprise ($59/user/mo, 10-user min) for CPQ, approval workflows, and API.
High send volume (>110/year): PandaDoc Business (unlimited docs, $49/seat/mo) or Better Proposals Business ($49/seat/mo, unlimited docs) to avoid per-doc overage fees.
All-in-one freelancer suite (proposals + invoicing + time tracking): Bonsai—contact vendor for pricing.
Proposify Basic if you send >10 proposals/month—overage fees at $0.50/send exceed the value of other tools' unlimited-seat or higher-cap plans.
Qwilr Enterprise for solopreneurs—the 10-user minimum ($590/mo annual) is overkill unless you're a 10+ person agency.
PandaDoc Free (60 docs/year) for active freelancers—110-doc Starter tier at $19/seat is the real entry point.
Better Proposals Starter if you need CRM integrations now—those are locked behind the $49/seat Business tier, same price as PandaDoc Business which bundles CRM access.
